Home / Work / Fuel Intelligence Hub

Energy · 2025 · SAAS Platform

Fuel Intelligence Hub

Intelligence platform that helps fuel suppliers optimize supply, pricing, and customer performance, driving customer retention and opening new revenue opportunities in the energy sector.

User Problem: Analysts spent hours reconciling fragmented fuel data, couldn’t make timely trading or operational decisions.

Business Goal: Launch a differentiated intelligence platform that helps fuel suppliers optimize supply, pricing, and customer performance, driving customer retention and opening new revenue opportunities in the energy sector

Context

Fuel suppliers often manage pricing, inventory, and customer performance across fragmented systems. Teams were forced to move between spreadsheets, BI exports, and internal tools to answer basic operational questions.

The opportunity was to create a single intelligence hub that supported daily decisions while also giving leadership a clearer picture of retention risk and revenue opportunities.

Placeholder for a full-width hero screenshot of the hub homepage, key KPI row, or an annotated overview of the information architecture.

Problem Framing (Text + Image Split)

What was not working

  • Analysts spent too much time assembling data before they could act on it.
  • Account teams lacked a shared view of customer performance trends.
  • Pricing and supply decisions were made without clear confidence signals.
  • Leadership reporting was reactive and manually compiled.

Placeholder for a workflow diagram, pain-point map, or service blueprint excerpt.

Design Direction (Alternating Image + Narrative Blocks)

1. Decision-first dashboard structure

I reorganized the experience around the core questions users needed to answer each day: where margin is at risk, which accounts need intervention, and where operational constraints could impact delivery.

Instead of presenting a flat analytics surface, the layout grouped metrics into action-oriented sections with progressive drill-downs.

Placeholder for an annotated interface showing hierarchy, KPI grouping, and drill-down entry points.

2. Customer health and opportunity signals

A new customer intelligence area surfaced retention and expansion indicators in one view. This helped account teams identify trend shifts earlier and coordinate with pricing/supply teams from the same signal set.

Placeholder for a customer detail panel with trend lines, risk flags, and opportunity signals.

Before / After Comparison (Two Image Placeholders)

BeforeAfter
Dense analytics with weak hierarchy and unclear next steps.Decision-first layout with clearer prioritization and progressive disclosure.

Process Snapshot (Text-led Block)

Approach

  1. Mapped recurring operational decisions and the data needed to support each one.
  2. Audited existing analytics surfaces and identified hierarchy / navigation failures.
  3. Prototyped alternative dashboard structures and tested comprehension with users.
  4. Sequenced delivery into phases so high-value workflows shipped first.

Collaboration

  • Product: prioritization, roadmap sequencing, success metrics
  • Engineering: data availability, performance constraints, implementation strategy
  • Operations / Account teams: validation of workflows, terminology, and decision timing

Outcomes (Metrics + Supporting Visual Placeholder)

  • Reduced time-to-insight for recurring operational reviews
  • Improved clarity of customer performance and retention risk signals
  • Fewer support questions tied to dashboard interpretation
  • Stronger alignment between account, pricing, and supply stakeholders

Placeholder for a metrics summary graphic, adoption chart, or release impact snapshot.
